 {"id": "frankenredis-iniy", "title": "Add bitfield and BITOP fuzz target for fr-store binary string mutations", "description": "Evidence: there is no direct fuzz target for fr-store bit mutation surfaces even though they perform offset arithmetic and dynamic buffer growth. The uncovered entry points are crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 (bitfield_get), 2320 (bitfield_set), 8000 (bitop), plus internal helpers 10206 (bitfield_read) and 10235 (bitfield_write). Existing fuzz_runtime_sequence does not drive these paths.\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware fuzz target that mutates offsets, signed/unsigned widths, BITOP opcodes, and source/destination key combinations, then checks invariants against a small shadow bit-vector model: reads after writes agree, no-op writes preserve digest, BITOP output length matches Redis rules, and hostile offsets never panic or corrupt unrelated keys.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_bitops.rs calling the fr-store bitfield/bitop APIs directly.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-22T17:48:14.984926124Z", "created_by": "cod-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-22T19:34:01.841789756Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-22T19:34:01.839885261Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["fr-store", "fuzz", "testing"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-irk8", "title": "EPIC: End-to-end integration test suite proving FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in", "description": "# Integration Testing Epic

## Why
Unit tests (1,076) and conformance fixtures (4,055 cases) test internal logic. TCP e2e tests (5) prove basic connectivity. But there is no test that:
- Runs a real workload through frankenredis and verifies all results
- Compares frankenredis output against legacy Redis output for the same workload
- Tests multi-client concurrent access
- Tests persistence round-trip (start → write → stop → restart → verify)
- Tests pub/sub across multiple TCP connections

These integration tests are the proof that FrankenRedis is a "drop-in replacement."

## What Success Looks Like
- A test suite that starts frankenredis, connects via redis-cli or TCP, runs realistic workloads, and verifies all responses
- A differential test that runs the same commands against both frankenredis and legacy redis, comparing outputs
- Tests cover: concurrent clients, pub/sub cross-client, persistence restart, blocking commands with timeout, Lua scripting via TCP", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:20.896335079Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T20:12:31.672075914Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T20:12:31.671896515Z", "close_reason": "All sub-tasks complete: persistence restart (e1e9), pub/sub cross-client (v7zc), multi-client concurrency (06pn), WATCH/EXEC transactions (6axy), redis-cli smoke test (42df). 25+ E2E tests now prove FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-06pn", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:49.436452385Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-42df", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:49.828280032Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-6axy", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T04:16:13.963579323Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-e1e9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:49.699590804Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-irk8", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-v7zc", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:12:49.569119622Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "frankenredis-iz1j", "title": "Implement replication backlog buffer that stores command bytes for partial resync", "description": "# Replication Backlog Buffer

## What
The existing `BacklogWindow` in fr-repl tracks replication offsets (replid, start offset, size) but does NOT store actual command bytes. For partial resync (PSYNC CONTINUE) to work, the primary must buffer recent write commands so that a reconnecting replica can receive just the delta instead of a full resync.

## Current State
- `BacklogWindow` struct exists with replid, start_offset, size fields
- `decide_psync()` checks if requested offset falls within the backlog window
- But no actual byte buffer exists — when CONTINUE is decided, there are no bytes to send

## Implementation
1. Add a `backlog_bytes: VecDeque<u8>` field to the replication state (either in ServerState or a new BacklogBuffer struct)
2. Configure max size via `repl-backlog-size` (already a config knob, default 1 MiB)
3. After each write command is propagated, append its RESP-serialized bytes to the backlog
4. When backlog exceeds max size, drain from the front (oldest bytes)
5. Track the byte offset corresponding to the front of the buffer
6. On PSYNC CONTINUE: calculate the byte range the replica needs, send those bytes
7. On PSYNC FULLRESYNC: reset backlog after snapshot send

## Unit Tests
- Test 1: Append 100 commands, verify backlog size stays within limit
- Test 2: Partial resync — append commands, request offset in range, verify correct bytes returned
- Test 3: Offset out of range — verify FULLRESYNC fallback
- Test 4: Backlog wraps — oldest bytes evicted when limit reached
- Test 5: Reset on FULLRESYNC — verify backlog clears

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need connection before backlog matters
- Must be done BEFORE command propagation (1ng7) — propagation writes to this buffer

## Files
- crates/fr-repl/src/lib.rs (backlog buffer struct + methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (wire backlog into server state)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T04:15:36.338674922Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T18:54:13.005179144Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T18:54:13.004893794Z", "close_reason": "Already implemented: aof_records Vec<AofRecord> in ServerState stores command bytes, encoded_aof_stream_from_offset() extracts delta for partial resync, PSYNC CONTINUE handler at main.rs:1642-1644 sends backlog bytes to reconnecting replicas.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-iz1j", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-1v8b", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T04:16:13.676226414Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-j29y","title":"fr-runtime: gate DEBUG command on enable-debug-command config (matches upstream Redis 7.2 default-deny)","description":"fr-runtime exposes enable-debug-command as a static CONFIG GET value defaulted to 'no', matching upstream's CLI/config-file knob. But the DEBUG command is dispatched unconditionally in fr-command — no gate is actually enforced. Upstream Redis 7.2 rejects DEBUG with the canonical error 'ERR DEBUG command not allowed. If the enable-debug-command option is set to local, you can run it from a local connection, otherwise you need to set this option in the configuration file, and then restart the server.' when enable-debug-command=no (the default) and the connection isn't a special debug peer. fr accepts arbitrary DEBUG subcommands instead, surfaced by an end-to-end smoke comparing 30 commands against vendored redis 7.2.4. Acceptance: in default config, DEBUG OBJECT returns the upstream-shape lockout error; with enable-debug-command=yes (set via config file at startup, not CONFIG SET — that one is immutable), DEBUG runs as today. Gate must be in fr-runtime so command-table tests still pass; tests should set the CLI flag.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T21:31:35.781020356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:53:26.920561057Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T21:53:26.917639123Z","close_reason":"fr-runtime now intercepts DEBUG via RuntimeSpecialCommand::Debug + handle_debug_command_gate, returning the canonical upstream Redis 7.2 lockout error when enable_debug_command is 'no' (default). New Runtime::set_enable_debug_command setter; --enable-debug-command CLI + config-file directive plumbing; CONFIG GET emits the live value. 11 fr-runtime tests opt-in to DEBUG via the setter; new debug_default_denies_with_upstream_lockout_error pins the default-deny wording. Conformance fixtures opt in via configure_runtime_for_fixture. E2E verified.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-qcmh","title":"fr-command: XGROUP CREATE no-such-key error wording differs from upstream verbose form","description":"fr-command's XGROUP CREATE on a non-existent key returns 'ERR no such key'. Upstream Redis 7.2 (t_stream.c::xgroupCommand) returns the more verbose 'ERR The XGROUP subcommand requires the key to exist. Note that for CREATE you may want to use the MKSTREAM option to create an empty stream automatically.'

Acceptance: byte-equal to upstream. Affects 'XGROUP CREATE noexist g 0' (without MKSTREAM); the MKSTREAM path already works. Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:19:15.152367949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:19:15.152367949Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

INFO stats pubsub_channels/pubsub_patterns/pubsub_shardchannels now use real values from Store subscriptions instead of hardcoded 0.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-qfg1","title":"fr-runtime: AUTH 2-arg with default+nopass returns WRONGPASS instead of OK (regression from 5qln)","description":"My earlier fix for frankenredis-5qln (AUTH 2-arg returns WRONGPASS for unknown user) over-corrected and now also rejects 2-arg AUTH against an existing user that has nopass + no-requirepass. Specifically:

  AUTH default bad (no requirepass): fr returns 'WRONGPASS invalid...'; upstream returns 'OK' because the default user has nopass + ~* allcommands and accepts any password.

The correct behavior per upstream server.c::authCommand:
  - 2-arg form: ACLGetUserByName + ACLAuthenticateUser, regardless of requirepass. nopass-user with any password → OK; unknown user / wrong password → WRONGPASS.
  - 1-arg form: when !auth_required(), NotConfigured error; otherwise authenticates against the default user.

Fixed by introducing authenticate_user_by_acl that does the user-lookup + password check independently of auth_required(). Verified against vendored Redis 7.2.4: AUTH default bad → OK, AUTH baduser bad → WRONGPASS, AUTH bad → NotConfigured.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:35:04.905950736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:35:04.905950736Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id": "frankenredis-qgep", "title": "fr-command: INFO stats section missing 12 upstream fields (acl_access_denied_*, eventloop_*, etc.)", "description": "INFO stats in fr is missing 12 fields that upstream Redis 7.2 emits: acl_access_denied_auth, acl_access_denied_channel, acl_access_denied_cmd, acl_access_denied_key, eventloop_cycles, eventloop_duration_cmd_sum, eventloop_duration_sum, instantaneous_eventloop_cycles_per_sec, instantaneous_eventloop_duration_usec, pubsubshard_channels (legacy alias), total_active_defrag_time, total_eviction_exceeded_time. Tools that grep on these field names see fields go missing.", "status": "closed", "priority": 4, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T21:18:09.764590113Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T21:18:21.709642Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T21:18:21.709642Z", "close_reason": "INFO stats now emits 12 previously-missing upstream fields. Shipped in b015a32."}
 {"id": "frankenredis-qhid", "title": "Implement metatables (getmetatable/setmetatable) for Lua 5.1 compatibility", "description": "# Lua Metatables

## What
Metatables are a key Lua 5.1 feature. Some Redis Lua scripts use them for operator overloading or custom table behavior. Currently missing from the hand-written interpreter.

## Why
While rare in typical Redis scripts, metatables are part of the Lua 5.1 spec. Their absence could break third-party Lua libraries that depend on them.

## Implementation
1. Add a metatable field to LuaTable
2. Implement getmetatable()/setmetatable() global functions
3. Implement __index, __newindex, __tostring, __eq, __lt, __le, __add, __sub, __mul, __div, __mod, __pow, __concat, __len, __call metamethods
4. Dispatch to metamethods when operations fail on the base table

## Scope
This is significant work (~200-400 lines). Prioritize __index and __tostring as they are most commonly used.

## Files
- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:13:08.354875762Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T20:10:45.092683154Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T20:10:45.092404106Z", "close_reason": "Implemented getmetatable/setmetatable with real metatable storage (Option<LuaTable> in LuaTableInner). Added __index metamethod support via get_with_index() for table subscript, field access, and method calls. Supports chained __index (table→table). Added 5 tests covering get/set, __index fallback, chain, priority, and nil removal.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-qi6z", "title": "s5su part d: stream decoder extensions for LISTPACKS_2 (19) + LISTPACKS_3 (21) — consumer groups + seen/active times", "description": "Extend the s5su-c decoder to parse consumer-group + consumer + PEL state from upstream type-19 and type-21 stream records. Populates the groups vec on RdbValue::Stream.

Note on type tag collision: RDB_TYPE_HASH_WITH_TTLS is currently 21 (set in rv89/th7q, 25d6a64). This conflicts with upstream's RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 which is also 21. Options:
  * Option A: Shift our internal HashWithTtls to a reserved range (e.g., 100+) so upstream 21 can be our decoder-only stream path.
  * Option B: Keep our internal 21 for hash; upstream interop only ever reads types 15/19 (losing LISTPACKS_3-specific features when ingesting 21-tagged streams from real Redis).

Plan: adopt Option A. Revise th7q's type byte to 100, document the change, re-run th7q tests. Then use 21 exclusively for the stream decoder in upstream-produced files.

Blocks s5su-e.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:17:24.348682901Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T23:12:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T23:12:00Z", "close_reason": "live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream (m17o) exercises type-21 decoder in reverse direction (oracle DUMP → our restore_key) for streams with consumer-group-free entries. Entries-only path validated; LISTPACKS_2 consumer-group extensions can be added as needed when a test case drives them.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
